title: Stock market news for June 29, 2026
headline: Dow closes above 52,000 for first time; S&P 500 +1.18%, Nasdaq +2.07%
lead_paragraph: The Dow Jones Industrial Average closed above 52,000 for the first time ever, gaining 306.63 points (+0.59%) to 52,182.74. The S&P 500 added 1.18% to 7,440.43, and the Nasdaq Composite surged 2.07% to 25,820.14. The rally was driven by Alphabet's debut in the Dow and a broader tech recovery.
extracted_text: Major Market Movements: Dow closed above 52,000 for first time (+306.63 pts, +0.59% to 52,182.74). S&P 500 +1.18% to 7,440.43. Nasdaq Composite +2.07% to 25,820.14. Top Movers: Alphabet (GOOGL) +4.82% on first day as Dow member. Comcast (CMCSA) +4.4% after announcing spin-off of NBCUniversal/Sky. Tesla (TSLA) +8.46%. Owens Corning (OC) +15.26% on unsolicited Carlisle offer. Marvell (MRVL) +4.12%, UBS raised target to $340. Charter (CHTR) +24% premarket on SpaceX mobile partnership talks. Oracle (ORCL) +3% rebounding from worst week since 2001. Rocket Lab (RKLB) +10% after acquiring Iridium. Notable Declines: TopBuild (BLD) -12%. BAT -2% premarket on 5,500 job cuts. Samsung -4.7%, SK Hynix -3.1% on $1.3T investment plans. AT&T, T-Mobile, CME, ICE, BSX, CPRT, ROL hit new 52-week lows. Geopolitics: US-Iran agreed to pause hostilities Sunday after weekend military exchanges. Oil: Brent +1.61% to $73.15, WTI +2.2% to $70.75. Gold: $4,068.10 (-0.68%). Supreme Court ruled 5-4 Trump cannot fire Fed Governor Lisa Cook. Treasury yields: 10Y 4.376% (+1bp), 2Y 4.102% (+1bp).

title: Asia Market Quick Take – 30 June, 2026
headline: Asia markets mixed; Nikkei +0.86%, Hang Seng -0.63%; USD/JPY hits 161.98
lead_paragraph: US equities rallied Monday: S&P 500 +1.2% to 7,440.43, Nasdaq 100 +2.3%, Dow topped 52,000 for first time. Asia session mixed with Nikkei +0.86% and Hang Seng -0.63%. USD stronger; USD/JPY briefly hit 161.98. Tehran ruled out negotiations at any level with the American side.
extracted_text: Macro: US-Iran talks in Doha uncertain; Tehran rules out negotiations. Japan IP +0.5% m/m (vs +1.1% exp), unemployment 2.5%. Dallas Fed flat, outlooks improved. UK shop price inflation steady at 1.2% y/y. Equities: US rally - S&P 500 +1.2%, Nasdaq 100 +2.3%, Dow >52k. Alphabet +4.8% on Dow debut. ASML +2.1%. HSI +1.6% led by Alibaba. After hours: Concentrix tumbled 25% on full-year outlook slash; AeroVironment jumped ~19% on strong Q4 beat. FX: USD stronger; USD/JPY briefly hit 161.98 (lowest since 1986). GBP top G10 performer. EUR softer toward 1.14. Commodities: Gold toward $4,000, worst month since June 2013, down >$500 in June. Brent ~$72.78. Copper -0.6%. Fixed Income: Treasuries mixed, 10Y ~4.37%. Key earnings this week: Tue - Nike, Constellation Brands. Wed - General Mills, FactSet.
discovered_by_query: global markets overnight Asia Europe June 30 2026

title: AeroVironment Q4 2026 Earnings: EPS $1.84 beats by 24%; stock +23% premarket
headline: AVAV Q4 EPS $1.84 vs $1.48 est, beats by 24.32%; stock +23% premarket to $171.26
summary: AeroVironment Q4 2026 Earnings: EPS $1.84 beats by 24%; stock +23% premarket
lead_paragraph: AeroVironment reported Q4 fiscal 2026 earnings on June 29, 2026, posting EPS of $1.84, beating analyst expectations of $1.48 by 24.32%. The stock jumped approximately 23% in pre-market trading.
extracted_text: Most Recent Earnings Q4 2026: Report Date June 29, 2026. Estimated EPS $1.48. Actual EPS $1.84 (Beat by +24.32%). Same quarter last year $1.61 EPS. Stock price: close $139.00, pre-market $171.26 (+23.21%). Prior quarters: Q3 2026 missed by -7.25%, Q2 2026 missed by -45.68%, Q1 2026 missed by -5.88%. Q4 marks first beat after three consecutive misses. Next report: Q1 2027 on Sep 8, 2026, est EPS $0.73 (sharp sequential drop).

title: Concentrix Reports Second Quarter 2026 Results
headline: CNXC Q2 revenue $2.46B (+1.9% YoY), non-GAAP EPS $2.63; full-year outlook cut, stock -25%
lead_paragraph: Concentrix reported Q2 fiscal 2026 results: revenue $2,462.5M (+1.9% YoY), non-GAAP diluted EPS $2.63 (-2.6% YoY). Operating income declined 35.7% to $95.4M. The company reduced its full-year outlook, sending shares down approximately 25% in after-hours trading.
extracted_text: Q2 FY2026 Financial Results: Revenue $2,462.5M (+1.9% YoY, constant currency +0.6%). Operating income $95.4M (-35.7% YoY), operating margin 3.9% (vs 6.1% prior year). Non-GAAP operating income $292.0M (-3.9%). Net income $55.3M (+31.4%). Non-GAAP diluted EPS $2.63 (-2.6%). Adjusted EBITDA $347.4M (-2.8%), margin 14.1%. Record Q2 operating cash flow $257.9M (+9.0%), adjusted FCF $242.3M (+21.0%). Revenue by segment: Technology & consumer electronics $624.2M (-6%), Retail/travel/e-commerce $640.8M (+10%), BFSI $432.4M (+13%), Healthcare $151.9M (-14%). Full Year FY2026 Guidance: Revenue $9.925-10.025B, Non-GAAP diluted EPS $10.83-11.18, Adjusted FCF $630-650M. Q3 FY2026 Guidance: Revenue $2.465-2.490B, Non-GAAP EPS $2.65-2.77. ix Suite deals up 400% YoY. Dividend $0.36/share payable Aug 4.

title: US Dollar strengthens; USD/JPY hits 162.28; DXY firm on rate expectations
headline: DXY firm; USD/JPY 162.28, EUR/USD 1.14, GBP/USD 1.324; yen weakest since 1986
summary: US Dollar strengthens; USD/JPY hits 162.28; DXY firm on rate expectations
lead_paragraph: The US dollar strengthened across the board in overnight trading, with USD/JPY briefly hitting 161.98 — its weakest level since 1986 — before settling at 162.28. EUR/USD traded at 1.14 and GBP/USD at 1.324.
extracted_text: FX rates (CNBC pre-market, ~5:50 AM ET June 30): USD/JPY 162.28 (+0.21%), EUR/USD 1.14 (-0.219%), GBP/USD 1.324 (-0.158%), USD/CHF 0.809 (+0.235%), USD/CAD 1.424 (+0.20%). Saxo overnight note: USD stronger, led by losses in commodity and risk-sensitive currencies. USD/JPY briefly hit 161.98. GBP top G10 performer. EUR softer toward 1.14. AUD set for biggest monthly fall since Dec 2024. Treasury yields: 10Y 4.371%, 2Y 4.106%. StoneX analysis: Treasury yields offer clearer explanation for recent dollar strength than DXY headline alone; short-dated yields climbing while 10Y and 30Y still lagging below 2026 highs.

title: Commodities: WTI $70.84, Brent $74.01, Gold $4,033; oil stabilizes after Iran ceasefire
headline: WTI +0.13% to $70.84, Brent $74.01; gold -0.13% to $4,033; silver +0.75%
summary: Commodities: WTI $70.84, Brent $74.01, Gold $4,033; oil stabilizes after Iran ceasefire
lead_paragraph: WTI crude traded at $70.84/barrel (+0.13%), extending Monday's 2.2% gain that followed the US-Iran agreement to pause hostilities and allow commercial vessel transit through the Strait of Hormuz. Brent crude at $74.01.
extracted_text: Commodities (CNBC pre-market, ~5:50 AM ET): Gold (Aug '26) $4,033.5 (-0.13%), Silver $58.61 (+0.75%), WTI Crude Oil $70.84 (+0.13%), Natural Gas $3.212 (+0.97%), RBOB Gasoline $3.042 (-0.64%). Brent crude $74.01 (+0.14%) per Trading Economics. Oil VIX 45.07 (-2.99%). Context: Oil rose Monday on US-Iran ceasefire - Brent +1.61% to $73.15, WTI +2.2% to $70.75. Morgan Stanley cut Q3 Brent forecast to $75. Gold toward $4,000, worst month since June 2013, down >$500 in June per Saxo. TD Securities: Gold may fall to $3,900 before rising to $5,300 by year-end 2026 on inflationary pressure. Copper -0.6%, tariff decision due today.

title: Markets News, June 29, 2026: Dow Closes at Record; S&P 500, Nasdaq Rebound After Tech Sell-Off
headline: Dow closes above 52,000 for first time; S&P 500 +1.2%, Nasdaq +2.1%; Comcast surges on split plan
summary: Markets News, June 29, 2026: Dow Closes at Record; S&P 500, Nasdaq Rebound After Tech Sell-Off
lead_paragraph: The Dow Jones Industrial Average closed above 52,000 for the first time, gaining 0.6%. The S&P 500 rose 1.2% and the Nasdaq Composite jumped 2.1%, both snapping five-session losing streaks. Technology stocks led the rebound after last week's rout.
extracted_text: Major US Indexes: Dow +0.6%, closed at record above 52,000 for first time. S&P 500 +1.2%, Nasdaq +2.1% — both snapping five-session losing streaks. Key Movers: Tesla (TSLA) +8.5%, Alphabet (GOOGL) +5% on Dow debut, SpaceX (SPCX) +7% on Nasdaq-100 inclusion July 7, Comcast (CMCSA) +4.5% on plan to split media/tech businesses. Charter (CHTR) +9% on SpaceX mobile partnership. Verizon (VZ) -6.5% leading decliners on Q2 loss guidance (00-800M). Amazon (AMZN) +3% on strong Prime Day estimates and AWS price hikes (+20% AI hardware rental). Strategy (MSTR) +12% after announcing .25B bitcoin monetization program. Iridium (IRDM) +22%, Rocket Lab (RKLB) +13% on acquisition deal at 4/share. Nike (NKE): expected to swing 8% post-earnings Tuesday; stock down 36% YTD. Commodities: WTI +1.8% to 0.45, Brent +1.6% to 3.15 on resumed US-Iran peace talks. Gold -1.6% to ,030. 10Y yield above 4.37%. DXY -0.3% to 101.10. Bitcoin ~0,400. Fed: Supreme Court blocked Trump from firing Fed Governor Cook (5-4). Chair Warsh shifting to shorter statements without forward guidance. June jobs report Thursday. Comcast Split: Spin off NBCUniversal from Comcast broadband/wireless. Brian Roberts co-CEO until split. SpaceX Nasdaq-100: Inclusion July 7, capped at 3x float weight (<1% index). New ETFs launching (QNDX, IQQ).
